facts.habitat: Open savannas, forest edges, pastures, and roadsides; often near cattle.
facts.diet: Omnivorous — carrion, insects, small reptiles, fruit, and ticks picked from livestock.
facts.range: Central and South America, from Costa Rica south to northern Argentina.
funFacts: Often perches on cattle and capybaras to pick off ticks and botfly larvae.
Despite being a falcon, it's a slow, opportunistic forager rather than an aerial hunter.
Juveniles have streaked brown plumage and look quite different from the pale-headed adults.
Its loud, screeching call is the source of its onomatopoeic local name 'chimachima'.
